170101.english: Paul, a servant of God, and an apostle of Jesus Christ, according to the faith of God's elect, and the acknowledging of the truth which is after godliness;
170101.cherokee: ᎠᏴ, ᏉᎳ, ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᎩᏅᏏᏙᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎠᎩᏅᏏᏛ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎤᏃᎯᏳᏒ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏧᏑᏰᏛ, ᎠᎴ [ᎾᏍᎩᏯ] ᏗᏓᏂᎸᎢᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎦᎾᏰᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᏥᎩ,
170101.phonetic: A-yv, Quo-la, U-ne-la-nv-hi a-gi-nv-si-do-hi, a-le Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv a-gi-nv-si-dv, na-s-gi-ya u-no-hi-yu-sv U-ne-la-nv-hi tsu-su-ye-dv, a-le [na-s-gi-ya] di-da-ni-lv-i-s-di ge-sv du-yu-go-dv ge-sv-i, na-s-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi ga-na-ye-hi-s-di ge-sv na-s-gi-ya tsi-gi,

170102.english: In hope of eternal life, which God, that cannot lie, promised before the world began;
170102.cherokee: ᏧᎵᏠᏯᏍᏗ ᎤᏚᎩ ᎬᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎬᏂᏛ ᎠᎵᏍᏆᏗᏍᎩ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎠᏩᏛᏗᏱ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏞᏩᏥᎪᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᏧᏚᎢᏍᏔᏁ ᎠᏏ ᎾᏓᎴᏂᏍᎬᎾ ᏥᎨᏎ ᎡᎶᎯ;
170102.phonetic: Tsu-li-tlo-ya-s-di u-du-gi gv-di ge-sv gv-ni-dv a-li-s-qua-di-s-gi ni-ge-sv-na a-wa-dv-di-yi, na-s-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi tle-wa-tsi-go-di ni-ge-sv-na tsu-du-i-s-ta-ne a-si na-da-le-ni-s-gv-na tsi-ge-se e-lo-hi;

170103.english: But hath in due times manifested his word through preaching, which is committed unto me according to the commandment of God our Saviour;
170103.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎾᎯᏳ ᎠᏎᎸᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏄᏩᏁᎴ ᎤᏤᎵ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎤᏮᏔᏁ ᎠᎵᏥᏙᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏴ ᎥᏆᎨᏅᏛᎯ ᏥᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎤᏁᏨ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎢᎩᏍᏕᎵᏍᎩ;
170103.phonetic: A-se-no na-hi-yu a-se-lv-hi ge-sv gv-ni-ge-sv nu-wa-ne-le u-tse-li ka-no-he-dv u-wv-ta-ne a-li-tsi-do-di ge-sv-i, na-s-gi a-yv v-qua-ge-nv-dv-hi tsi-gi, na-s-gi-ya u-ne-tsv U-ne-la-nv-hi I-gi-s-de-li-s-gi;

170104.english: To Titus, mine own son after the common faith: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ our Saviour.
170104.cherokee: ᏫᎬᏲᏪᎳᏏ, ᏓᏓᏏ, ᎠᏇᏥᏯᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎾᏂᎥ ᎠᏃᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩ ᎤᏃᎯᏳᏒᎢ; ᎬᏩᎦᏘᏯ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏣᏛᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏅᏩᏙᎯᏯᏛ, ᏅᏓᏳᎾᎵᏍᎪᎸᏔᏅᎯ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᎦᏴᎵᎨᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎢᎩᏍᏕᎵᏍᎩ, ᏕᏣᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎮᏍᏗ.
170104.phonetic: Wi-gv-yo-we-la-si, Da-da-si, a-que-tsi-ya-i, na-s-gi-ya na-ni-v a-no-hi-yu-hv-s-gi u-no-hi-yu-sv-i; gv-wa-ga-ti-ya u-da-do-li-s-di ge-sv, a-le u-da-do-li-tsa-dv-i, a-le nv-wa-do-hi-ya-dv, nv-da-yu-na-li-s-go-lv-ta-nv-hi U-ne-la-nv-hi A-ga-yv-li-ge-i, a-le U-gv-wi-yu-hi tsi-sa ga-lo-ne-dv I-gi-s-de-li-s-gi, de-tsa-ka-hna-wa-di-do-he-s-di.

170105.english: For this cause left I thee in Crete, that thou shouldest set in order the things that are wanting, and ordain elders in every city, as I had appointed thee:
170105.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᏅᏓᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬᎩ ᏟᏗᏱ ᏨᏓᎬᏯᎧᎯᏴᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎢᏨᏁᎵᏓᏍᏗᏱ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᎤᏃᏒᏅᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏘᎪᏗᏱ ᏗᎨᎦᏁᎶᏗ ᏕᎦᏚᏩᏗᏒᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎢᏣᏛᏁᏗᏱ ᎬᏁᏤᎸᎢ;
170105.phonetic: Hi-a nv-da-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv-gi Tli-di-yi tsv-da-gv-ya-ka-hi-yv-gi, na-s-gi o-s-dv i-tsv-ne-li-da-s-di-yi na-s-gi tsu-da-le-nv-dv u-no-sv-nv-i, a-le ti-go-di-yi di-ge-ga-ne-lo-di de-ga-du-wa-di-sv-i, na-s-gi-ya i-tsa-dv-ne-di-yi gv-ne-tse-lv-i;

170106.english: If any be blameless, the husband of one wife, having faithful children not accused of riot or unruly.
170106.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏃ ᎩᎶ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎾᎫᎢᏍᏛᎾ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᏌᏉ ᎠᏓᏰᎮᏍᏗ, ᎠᏃᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩ ᏚᏪᎧᎮᏍᏗ ᏧᏪᏥ, ᏂᎨᏥᎳᏫᏎᎲᎾ ᏄᎾᏁᎸᎾ ᎤᎾᎴᏂᏙᎸᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏂᏚᎾᏓᏁᎶᏛᎾ.
170106.phonetic: I-yu-no gi-lo go-hu-s-di na-gu-i-s-dv-na ge-se-s-di, sa-quo a-da-ye-he-s-di, a-no-hi-yu-hv-s-gi du-we-ka-he-s-di tsu-we-tsi, ni-ge-tsi-la-wi-se-hv-na nu-na-ne-lv-na u-na-le-ni-do-lv-i, a-le ni-du-na-da-ne-lo-dv-na.

170107.english: For a bishop must be blameless, as the steward of God; not selfwilled, not soon angry, not given to wine, no striker, not given to filthy lucre;
170107.cherokee: ᎠᏥᎦᏘᏗᏍᏗᏰᏃ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎾᎫᎢᏍᏛᎾ ᏱᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎢᏳᏩᎿᎭᏕᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ; ᎥᏝ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎠᏓᏅᏖᏍᎬ ᎤᏟ ᎤᏰᎸᎯ, ᎥᏝ ᎤᎿᎭᎸᏣᏘ, ᎥᏝ ᎩᎦᎨ-ᎠᏗᏔᏍᏗ ᎤᎦᎾᏏᏍᎩ, ᎥᏝ ᎠᏓᏛᏂᎯ, ᎥᏝ ᏧᎬᏩᎶᏗ ᎤᎬᎥᏍᎩ.
170107.phonetic: A-tsi-ga-ti-di-s-di-ye-no go-hu-s-di na-gu-i-s-dv-na yi-gi, na-s-gi-ya o-s-dv i-yu-wa-hna-de-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li-ga; v-tla u-wa-sv a-da-nv-te-s-gv u-tli u-ye-lv-hi, v-tla u-hna-lv-tsa-ti, v-tla gi-ga-ge--a-di-ta-s-di u-ga-na-si-s-gi, v-tla a-da-dv-ni-hi, v-tla tsu-gv-wa-lo-di u-gv-v-s-gi.

170108.english: But a lover of hospitality, a lover of good men, sober, just, holy, temperate;
170108.cherokee: ᎤᏓᏍᏆᏂᎪᏙᏗᏱᏍᎩᏂ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎤᏰᎸᎯ, ᎣᏍᏛ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎨᏳᎯ, ᎤᏴᏍᏕᏍᎩ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ, ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᎢᏯᏛᏁᎯ, ᎾᏍᎦᏅᎾ, ᎤᎵᏏᎾᏍᏗ;
170108.phonetic: U-da-s-qua-ni-go-do-di-yi-s-gi-ni o-si-yu u-ye-lv-hi, o-s-dv ge-sv u-ge-yu-hi, u-yv-s-de-s-gi ni-ge-sv-na, du-yu-go-dv i-ya-dv-ne-hi, na-s-ga-nv-na, u-li-si-na-s-di;

170109.english: Holding fast the faithful word as he hath been taught, that he may be able by sound doctrine both to exhort and to convince the gainsayers.
170109.cherokee: ᎠᏍᏓᏯ ᏧᏂᏴᎯ ᏄᏓᎵᏓᏍᏛᎾ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎠᎨᏲᏅᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏅᏓᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᏰᎵᏉ ᏗᎬᏩᏬᏁᏙᏗ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏗᏕᏲᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎬᏗᏍᎬᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᎾᏙᎯᏳᎾᏁᏗ ᎢᎬᏩᏁᏗ ᏗᏂᎦᏘᎴᎩ ᎨᏒᎢ.
170109.phonetic: A-s-da-ya tsu-ni-yv-hi nu-da-li-da-s-dv-na ka-no-he-dv na-s-gi-ya a-ge-yo-nv-i, na-s-gi nv-da-yu-li-s-do-di-yi ye-li-quo di-gv-wa-wo-ne-do-di o-s-dv di-de-yo-di ge-sv gv-di-s-gv-i; a-le gv-wa-na-do-hi-yu-na-ne-di i-gv-wa-ne-di di-ni-ga-ti-le-gi ge-sv-i.

170110.english: For there are many unruly and vain talkers and deceivers, specially they of the circumcision:
170110.cherokee: ᎤᏂᏣᏔᏰᏃ ᏂᏚᎾᏓᏁᎶᏛᎾ, ᎠᏎᏉᏉ ᎠᏂᏬᏂᏍᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᏠᎾᏍᏗ, ᎨᎤᎬᏫᏳᎭ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᏗᎨᏥᎤᏍᏕᏎᎸᎯ;
170110.phonetic: U-ni-tsa-ta-ye-no ni-du-na-da-ne-lo-dv-na, a-se-quo-quo a-ni-wo-ni-s-gi, a-le u-na-tlo-na-s-di, ge-u-gv-wi-yu-ha na-s-gi na di-ge-tsi-u-s-de-se-lv-hi;

170111.english: Whose mouths must be stopped, who subvert whole houses, teaching things which they ought not, for filthy lucre's sake.
170111.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᏂᎰᎵ ᎠᏎ ᏗᎨᏥᏍᏚᏁᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᏂᎥ ᏏᏓᏁᎸᎯ ᎠᏁᎯ ᏗᏂᎦᏔᎲᏍᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᏓᎾᏕᏲᎲᏍᎦ ᏂᏚᏳᎪᏛᎾ ᏥᎩ ᏧᎾᏕᏲᏗᏱ, ᏧᎬᏩᎶᏗ ᎦᏓᎭ ᎤᏂᎬᎥᏍᎬ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ.
170111.phonetic: Na-s-gi di-ni-ho-li a-se di-ge-tsi-s-du-ne-di, na-s-gi na-ni-v si-da-ne-lv-hi a-ne-hi di-ni-ga-ta-hv-s-gi, na-s-gi tsi-da-na-de-yo-hv-s-ga ni-du-yu-go-dv-na tsi-gi tsu-na-de-yo-di-yi, tsu-gv-wa-lo-di ga-da-ha u-ni-gv-v-s-gv i-yu-s-di.

170112.english: One of themselves, even a prophet of their own, said, The Cretians are always liars, evil beasts, slow bellies.
170112.cherokee: ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎤᏅᏒᏉ ᎠᏁᎲ ᎡᎯ, ᎠᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᎤᏅᏒᏉ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎦ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᏟᏗᏱ ᎠᏁᎯ ᎠᏂᏰᎪᎩ ᎨᏐ ᏂᎪᎯᎸᎢ, ᎤᏂᏲ ᎢᎾᎨ ᎠᏁᎯ, ᏧᏍᎦᏃᎸ ᏧᏂᏍᏉᎵᏱ.
170112.phonetic: Gi-lo i-yu-s-di u-nv-sv-quo a-ne-hv e-hi, a-do-le-ho-s-gi u-nv-sv-quo u-na-tse-li-ga, hi-a nu-we-se-i, Tli-di-yi a-ne-hi a-ni-ye-go-gi ge-so ni-go-hi-lv-i, u-ni-yo i-na-ge a-ne-hi, tsu-s-ga-no-lv tsu-ni-s-quo-li-yi.

170113.english: This witness is true. Wherefore rebuke them sharply, that they may be sound in the faith;
170113.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎦᏪᏛ ᏥᎩ ᎤᎪᎯᏳᎭ.  ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᏍᏓᏯ ᏕᎯᎬᏍᎪᎸᏅᎭ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎪᎸ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᎤᏃᎯᏳᏒᎢ;
170113.phonetic: Hi-a na-s-gi i-ga-we-dv tsi-gi u-go-hi-yu-ha.  Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di a-s-da-ya de-hi-gv-s-go-lv-nv-ha, na-s-gi u-go-lv ni-ge-sv-na i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi u-no-hi-yu-sv-i;

170114.english: Not giving heed to Jewish fables, and commandments of men, that turn from the truth.
170114.cherokee: ᏄᎾᎦᏌᏯᏍᏛᎾᏉ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎤᏂᏃᎮᎸᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏴᏫᏉ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᎨᏒ ᏗᏂᎪᎸᏍᎩ ᎤᏂᏁᏨᎯ.
170114.phonetic: Nu-na-ga-sa-ya-s-dv-na-quo i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi A-ni-tsu-si u-ni-no-he-lv-di ge-sv-i, a-le yv-wi-quo na-s-gi du-yu-go-dv ge-sv di-ni-go-lv-s-gi u-ni-ne-tsv-hi.

170115.english: Unto the pure all things are pure: but unto them that are defiled and unbelieving is nothing pure; but even their mind and conscience is defiled.
170115.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᎦᎸᏛ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᏂᎦᏗᏳ ᏧᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᎦᎸᎡᎸᎯ ᎨᏐᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩᏍᎩᏂ Ꮎ ᎦᏓᎭ ᎢᏳᎾᎵᏍᏔᏅᎯ ᎠᎴ ᏄᏃᎯᏳᏒᎾ, ᎥᏝ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᎦᎸᎡᎸᎯ ᏱᎨᏐᎢ; ᎾᏍᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᏚᎾᏓᏅᏛ ᎠᎴ ᏧᏄᎪᏙᏗᏱ ᎦᏓᎭ ᎢᏗᎬᏁᎸᎯ.
170115.phonetic: Na-s-gi na u-na-da-nv-ga-lv-dv ge-sv-i, ni-ga-di-yu tsu-da-le-nv-dv u-na-da-nv-ga-lv-e-lv-hi ge-so-i; na-s-gi-s-gi-ni na ga-da-ha i-yu-na-li-s-ta-nv-hi a-le nu-no-hi-yu-sv-na, v-tla go-hu-s-di u-na-da-nv-ga-lv-e-lv-hi yi-ge-so-i; na-s-quo-s-gi-ni du-na-da-nv-dv a-le tsu-nu-go-do-di-yi ga-da-ha i-di-gv-ne-lv-hi.

170116.english: They profess that they know God; but in works they deny him, being abominable, and disobedient, and unto every good work reprobate.
170116.cherokee: ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᎾᏅᏁᎭ ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎯᏳ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᏚᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎲ ᎢᎬᏩᏓᏱᎭ, ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᎭ ᎤᏂᏂᏆᏘᏍᏗᏳ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏃᎯᏳᎯᏍᏛ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎨᏥᏐᏅᎢᏍᏔᏅᎯ.
170116.phonetic: Gv-ni-ge-sv na-nv-ne-ha a-ni-ga-ta-hi-yu ge-sv U-ne-la-nv-hi; a-se-no du-ni-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hv i-gv-wa-da-yi-ha, nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-ha u-ni-ni-qua-ti-s-di-yu ge-sv-i, a-le u-no-hi-yu-hi-s-dv ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv-i; a-le ni-ga-v o-s-dv di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di ge-sv ge-tsi-so-nv-i-s-ta-nv-hi.

170201.english: But speak thou the things which become sound doctrine:
170201.cherokee: ᏂᎯᏍᎩᏂ ᎯᏃᎮᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏗᏕᏲᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎵᎶᎲᏍᎩ;
170201.phonetic: Ni-hi-s-gi-ni hi-no-he-s-ge-s-di na-s-gi o-s-dv di-de-yo-di ge-sv u-li-lo-hv-s-gi;

170202.english: That the aged men be sober, grave, temperate, sound in faith, in charity, in patience.
170202.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᎾᏛᏐᏅᎯ ᏧᏂᏴᏍᏕᏍᎩ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎢᏳᎾᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ, ᎨᏥᎸᏉᏗ, ᎤᎾᎵᏏᎾᎯᏍᏗ, ᏄᎪᎸᎾ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏃᎯᏳᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᏓᎨᏳᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᏅᏂᏗᏳ ᎨᏒᎢ.
170202.phonetic: Na-s-gi tsu-na-dv-so-nv-hi tsu-ni-yv-s-de-s-gi ni-ge-sv-na i-yu-na-li-s-do-di-yi, ge-tsi-lv-quo-di, u-na-li-si-na-hi-s-di, nu-go-lv-na ge-sv u-no-hi-yu-sv-i, a-le u-na-da-ge-yu-sv-i, a-le di-nv-ni-di-yu ge-sv-i.

170203.english: The aged women likewise, that they be in behaviour as becometh holiness, not false accusers, not given to much wine, teachers of good things;
170203.cherokee: ᎠᏂᎨᏴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎠᏂᎦᏴᎵᎨᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏚᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎲ ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᎢᏯᏛᏁᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎵᎶᎲᏍᎩ ᏱᎩ, ᎤᏐᏅ ᎠᎾᏓᏃᎮᏍᎩ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ, ᎩᎦᎨ-ᎠᏗᏔᏍᏗ ᎤᏂᎦᎾᏏᏍᎩ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ, ᎣᏍᏛ ᎨᏒ ᏗᎾᏕᏲᎲᏍᎩ;
170203.phonetic: A-ni-ge-yv na-s-quo a-ni-ga-yv-li-ge-i, na-s-gi du-ni-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hv du-yu-go-dv i-ya-dv-ne-di ge-sv u-li-lo-hv-s-gi yi-gi, u-so-nv a-na-da-no-he-s-gi ni-ge-sv-na, gi-ga-ge--a-di-ta-s-di u-ni-ga-na-si-s-gi ni-ge-sv-na, o-s-dv ge-sv di-na-de-yo-hv-s-gi;

170204.english: That they may teach the young women to be sober, to love their husbands, to love their children,
170204.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᎬᏩᏁᏲᏗ ᏱᎩ ᎠᏂᏫᎾᎨᏌᏂ ᎠᏂᎨᏴ ᎤᎾᎵᏏᎾᎯᏍᏗ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ, ᏧᏂᎨᏳᎯ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᏗᎬᏩᏂᏰᎯ, ᏧᏂᎨᏳᎯ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᏧᏁᏥ,
170204.phonetic: Na-s-gi di-gv-wa-ne-yo-di yi-gi a-ni-wi-na-ge-sa-ni a-ni-ge-yv u-na-li-si-na-hi-s-di i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi, tsu-ni-ge-yu-hi i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi di-gv-wa-ni-ye-hi, tsu-ni-ge-yu-hi i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi tsu-ne-tsi,

170205.english: To be discreet, chaste, keepers at home, good, obedient to their own husbands, that the word of God be not blasphemed.
170205.cherokee: ᎤᎾᎵᏏᎾᎯᏍᏗ ᎢᏳᎾᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ, ᎠᏁᏯᏔᎯ, ᏚᏁᏅᏒᏉ ᎠᏁᏙᎯ, ᎠᏃᏍᏛ, ᏧᏃᎯᏳᎯ ᎤᏅᏒ ᏗᎬᏩᏂᏰᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏅᏓᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵ ᎦᎬᏩᏂᏐᏢᏙᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ.
170205.phonetic: U-na-li-si-na-hi-s-di i-yu-na-li-s-do-di-yi, a-ne-ya-ta-hi, du-ne-nv-sv-quo a-ne-do-hi, a-no-s-dv, tsu-no-hi-yu-hi u-nv-sv di-gv-wa-ni-ye-hi, na-s-gi nv-da-yu-li-s-do-di-yi ka-no-he-dv U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li ga-gv-wa-ni-so-tlv-do-di-yi ni-ge-sv-na.

170206.english: Young men likewise exhort to be sober minded.

170207.english: In all things shewing thyself a pattern of good works: in doctrine shewing uncorruptness, gravity, sincerity,
170207.cherokee: ᏂᎦᎥ ᏧᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᎨᏒ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏅᏁᎮᏍᏗ ᏗᎦᏰᏣᏟᎶᏍᏙᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ; ᏗᏕᏂᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ ᎤᏲ ᏄᏓᏑᏴᎾ, ᎬᎸᏉᏗᏳ, ᏄᏠᎾᏍᏛᎾ,
170207.phonetic: Ni-ga-v tsu-da-le-nv-dv ge-sv gv-ni-ge-sv nv-ne-he-s-di di-ga-ye-tsa-tli-lo-s-do-di ge-sv o-s-dv di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di ge-sv-i; di-de-ni-di ge-sv u-gv-wa-li u-yo nu-da-su-yv-na, gv-lv-quo-di-yu, nu-tlo-na-s-dv-na,

170208.english: Sound speech, that cannot be condemned; that he that is of the contrary part may be ashamed, having no evil thing to say of you.
170208.cherokee: ᎯᏬᏂᏍᎬ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏱᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᎪᏁᎶᎯᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ; ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᏣᏡᏗᏍᎩ ᎬᏩᏕᎰᎯᏍᏗ ᏱᎩ ᏄᎲᏅ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎤᏐᏅ ᎶᏃᎮᏗᏱ.
170208.phonetic: Hi-wo-ni-s-gv o-s-dv yi-gi na-s-gi gv-go-ne-lo-hi-s-di ni-ge-sv-na; na-s-gi na tsa-tlu-di-s-gi gv-wa-de-ho-hi-s-di yi-gi nu-hv-nv ge-sv i-yu-s-di go-hu-s-di u-so-nv lo-no-he-di-yi.

170209.english: Exhort servants to be obedient unto their own masters, and to please them well in all things; not answering again;
170209.cherokee: ᎨᏥᏅᏏᏓᏍᏗ ᏕᎯᏁᏤᎮᏍᏗ ᏧᏃᎯᏳᏗᏱ ᎤᏅᏒ ᏗᎬᏩᏂᏅᏏᏙᎯ, ᏂᎦᎥᏉ ᎾᎾᏛᏁᎲ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎤᏂᏰᎸᏗ ᏧᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗᏱ; ᏔᎵᏁᏉ ᏅᏗᏂᏁᎬᎾ.
170209.phonetic: Ge-tsi-nv-si-da-s-di de-hi-ne-tse-he-s-di tsu-no-hi-yu-di-yi u-nv-sv di-gv-wa-ni-nv-si-do-hi, ni-ga-v-quo na-na-dv-ne-hv o-s-dv u-ni-ye-lv-di tsu-ni-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di-yi; ta-li-ne-quo nv-di-ni-ne-gv-na.

170210.english: Not purloining, but shewing all good fidelity; that they may adorn the doctrine of God our Saviour in all things.
170210.cherokee: ᎾᏂᏃᏍᎩᏍᎬᎾ, ᎬᏂᎨᏒᏍᎩᏂ ᎾᏅᏁᎮᏍᏗ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏄᏓᎵᏓᏍᏛᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏃᏚᎢᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᏂᎦᎥᏉ ᎾᎾᏛᏁᎲ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᏕᏲᏅᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎢᎩᏍᏕᎵᏍᎩ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ.
170210.phonetic: Na-ni-no-s-gi-s-gv-na, gv-ni-ge-sv-s-gi-ni na-nv-ne-he-s-di ni-ga-v o-s-dv nu-da-li-da-s-dv-na ge-sv-i; na-s-gi u-no-du-i-s-do-di-yi ni-ga-v-quo na-na-dv-ne-hv na-s-gi di-de-yo-nv-hi ge-sv U-ne-la-nv-hi I-gi-s-de-li-s-gi u-tse-li-ga.

170211.english: For the grace of God that bringeth salvation hath appeared to all men,
170211.cherokee: ᎬᏩᎦᏘᏯᏰᏃ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏓᏍᏕᎵᏍᎩ ᎾᏂᎥ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏂᎾᏄᎪᏤᎸ,
170211.phonetic: Gv-wa-ga-ti-ya-ye-no u-da-do-li-s-di ge-sv U-ne-la-nv-hi na-s-gi a-da-s-de-li-s-gi na-ni-v yv-wi u-ni-na-nu-go-tse-lv,

170212.english: Teaching us that, denying ungodliness and worldly lusts, we should live soberly, righteously, and godly, in this present world;
170212.cherokee: ᎢᎨᏲᎲᏍᎦ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏓᏓᏱᎲ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏗᏁᎶᏙᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ ᎠᎴ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎡᎯ ᎠᏚᎸᏅᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎢᎦᎵᏏᎾᎯᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᏚᏳᎪᏛᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎦᎸᏉᏙᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏱᏓᎴᏂᏙᎭ ᎠᏂ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ;
170212.phonetic: I-ge-yo-hv-s-ga, na-s-gi i-da-da-yi-hv U-ne-la-nv-hi di-ne-lo-do-di ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv-i a-le e-lo-hi e-hi a-du-lv-nv-di ge-sv-i, i-ga-li-si-na-hi-s-di a-le du-yu-go-dv-i, a-le U-ne-la-nv-hi ga-lv-quo-do-di ge-sv yi-da-le-ni-do-ha a-ni e-lo-hi ge-sv-i;

170213.english: Looking for that blessed hope, and the glorious appearing of the great God and our Saviour Jesus Christ;
170213.cherokee: ᏱᏗᎦᏘᏯ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎤᏚᎩ ᎬᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᏄᏛᏁᏍᏗ ᎦᎾᏄᎪᏥᎸ ᎦᎸᏉᏗ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎢᎩᏍᏕᎵᏍᎩ ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ;
170213.phonetic: Yi-di-ga-ti-ya o-s-dv u-du-gi gv-di ge-sv-i, na-s-gi ga-lv-quo-di-yu nu-dv-ne-s-di ga-na-nu-go-tsi-lv ga-lv-quo-di U-ne-la-nv-hi a-le I-gi-s-de-li-s-gi Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv;

170214.english: Who gave himself for us, that he might redeem us from all iniquity, and purify unto himself a peculiar people, zealous of good works.
170214.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏴ ᏥᏕᎦᏓᎵᏲᎯᏎᎴᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏅᏓᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᎢᎦᎫᏴᏗᏱ ᎢᎫᏓᎴᏍᏗᏱ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎠᏍᎦᏅᎢᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᏅᎦᎸᏗᏱ ᎤᏩᏒ ᏧᏤᎵ ᎢᏧᏩᏁᏗᏱ ᎤᎾᏓᏤᎵᏛ ᏴᏫ, ᎤᎵᏂᎩᏛ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏘ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏧᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗᏱ.
170214.phonetic: Na-s-gi a-yv tsi-de-ga-da-li-yo-hi-se-le-i, na-s-gi nv-da-yu-li-s-do-di-yi i-ga-gu-yv-di-yi i-gu-da-le-s-di-yi ni-ga-v a-s-ga-nv-i-s-di ge-sv-i, a-le tsu-nv-ga-lv-di-yi u-wa-sv tsu-tse-li i-tsu-wa-ne-di-yi u-na-da-tse-li-dv yv-wi, u-li-ni-gi-dv u-na-da-nv-ti o-s-dv tsu-ni-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di-yi.

170215.english: These things speak, and exhort, and rebuke with all authority. Let no man despise thee.
170215.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏍᏕᏍᏗ ᎯᏃᎮᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᏕᎯᏬᏁᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᏕᎯᎬᏍᎪᎸᎥᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎲᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᏂᎦᎥ ᏣᏒᎦᎸᎢ.  ᎲᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᏞᏍᏗ ᎩᎶ ᎡᏍᎦ ᏱᏣᏰᎸᏎᏍᏗ.
170215.phonetic: Na-s-gi hi-a nu-s-de-s-di hi-no-he-s-ge-s-di, a-le de-hi-wo-ne-di-s-ge-s-di, a-le de-hi-gv-s-go-lv-v-s-ge-s-di hv-di-s-ge-s-di ni-ga-v tsa-sv-ga-lv-i.  Hv-di-s-ge-s-di Tle-s-di gi-lo e-s-ga yi-tsa-ye-lv-se-s-di.

170301.english: Put them in mind to be subject to principalities and powers, to obey magistrates, to be ready to every good work,
170301.cherokee: ᏕᎭᏅᏓᏗᏍᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᏧᏃᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏄᏂᎬᏫᏳᏌᏕᎩ ᎠᎴ ᏗᎨᎦᏁᎶᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎤᏃᎯᏳᎯᏍᏛ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᏂᎪᎯᎸᏉ ᎤᎾᏛᏅᎢᏍᏗ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏂᎦᎥᏉ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏧᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗᏱ.
170301.phonetic: De-ha-nv-da-di-s-di-s-ge-s-di tsu-no-hi-yu-di ge-sv nu-ni-gv-wi-yu-sa-de-gi a-le di-ge-ga-ne-lo-di ge-sv-i, u-no-hi-yu-hi-s-dv i-yu-li-s-do-di ge-sv-i, ni-go-hi-lv-quo u-na-dv-nv-i-s-di i-yu-li-s-do-di ge-sv ni-ga-v-quo o-s-dv tsu-ni-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di-yi.

170302.english: To speak evil of no man, to be no brawlers, but gentle, shewing all meekness unto all men.
170302.cherokee: ᎩᎶ ᎤᏐᏅ ᎤᏂᏃᎮᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ, ᏧᎾᏘᏲᏌᏘ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎢᏳᎾᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ, ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏘᏍᎩᏂ ᎢᏳᎾᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ, ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᏅᏁᏗᏱ ᎾᏂᎥᏉ ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎲ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏘᏳ ᎨᏒᎢ.
170302.phonetic: Gi-lo u-so-nv u-ni-no-he-di-yi ni-ge-sv-na, tsu-na-ti-yo-sa-ti ni-ge-sv-na i-yu-na-li-s-do-di-yi, u-na-da-nv-ti-s-gi-ni i-yu-na-li-s-do-di-yi, gv-ni-ge-sv i-yu-nv-ne-di-yi na-ni-v-quo a-ni-ga-ta-hv u-tsa-ti u-na-da-nv-ti-yu ge-sv-i.

170303.english: For we ourselves also were sometimes foolish, disobedient, deceived, serving divers lusts and pleasures, living in malice and envy, hateful, and hating one another.
170303.cherokee: ᎢᎬᏒᏰᏃ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎢᎸᎯᏳ ᏥᎨᏒ ᎢᎩᏁᎫ ᎨᏒᎩ, ᏂᎪᎯᏳᏒᎾ, ᎢᎩᎵᏓᏍᏔᏅᎯ, ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏕᏓᏛᏁᎲᎩ ᏧᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᎠᏚᎸᏅᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎴ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎠᏓᏓᏅᏓᏗᏍᏗᏍᎩ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎤᏲ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏛᏳᎨᏗ ᎢᏕᎲᎩ, ᎢᎩᏂᏆᏘᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᏓᏓᏂᏆᏘᎯ ᎨᏒᎩ.
170303.phonetic: I-gv-sv-ye-no na-s-quo i-lv-hi-yu tsi-ge-sv i-gi-ne-gu ge-sv-gi, ni-go-hi-yu-sv-na, i-gi-li-da-s-ta-nv-hi, go-hu-s-di de-da-dv-ne-hv-gi tsu-da-le-nv-dv a-du-lv-nv-di ge-sv a-le o-s-dv a-da-da-nv-da-di-s-di-s-gi ge-sv-i, u-yo ge-sv a-le a-dv-yu-ge-di i-de-hv-gi, i-gi-ni-qua-ti-s-di, a-le di-da-da-ni-qua-ti-hi ge-sv-gi.

170304.english: But after that the kindness and love of God our Saviour toward man appeared,
170304.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎢᎩᏍᏕᎵᏍᎩ ᎤᏓᏅᏘᏳ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎴ ᏴᏫ ᏚᎨᏳᏒ ᎤᎦᏄᎪᏨ,
170304.phonetic: A-se-no hna-quo U-ne-la-nv-hi I-gi-s-de-li-s-gi u-da-nv-ti-yu ge-sv a-le yv-wi du-ge-yu-sv u-ga-nu-go-tsv,

170305.english: Not by works of righteousness which we have done, but according to his mercy he saved us, by the washing of regeneration, and renewing of the Holy Ghost;
170305.cherokee: ᎥᏝ ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎬᏗ ᎠᏴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏕᎩᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎸᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏣᏘᏳ ᎨᏒ ᎢᎩᏍᏕᎸᎲᎩ ᎤᏮᏔᏅᎩ ᏔᎵᏁ ᎠᏕᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ ᎠᏙᏑᎴᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎤᏓᏁᏟᏴᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ;
170305.phonetic: V-tla du-yu-go-dv di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di ge-sv gv-di a-yv na-s-gi de-gi-lv-wi-s-da-ne-lv-i, na-s-gi-ya-s-gi-ni u-da-do-li-tsa-ti-yu ge-sv i-gi-s-de-lv-hv-gi u-wv-ta-nv-gi ta-li-ne a-de-di ge-sv u-gv-wa-li a-do-su-le-di-yi, a-le Ga-lv-quo-di-yu A-da-nv-do u-da-ne-tli-yv-s-di ge-sv-i;

170306.english: Which he shed on us abundantly through Jesus Christ our Saviour;
170306.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏧᏈᏍᏗ ᏥᎩᏐᏅᏰᎵ, ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎢᎩᏍᏕᎵᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏩᏂᏌᏛ;
170306.phonetic: Na-s-gi u-tsu-qui-s-di tsi-gi-so-nv-ye-li, Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv I-gi-s-de-li-s-gi i-yu-wa-ni-sa-dv;

170307.english: That being justified by his grace, we should be made heirs according to the hope of eternal life.
170307.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᏩᎦᏘᏯ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏗᎦᏚᏓᎴᏍᏔᏅᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎤᎵᏍᏆᏗᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎬᏂᏛ ᎢᎦᏤᎵ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎤᏚᎩ ᎢᎬᏒᎢ.
170307.phonetic: Na-s-gi gv-wa-ga-ti-ya u-da-do-li-s-di ge-sv di-ga-du-da-le-s-ta-nv-hi ge-sv-i, na-s-gi na-s-gi-ya u-li-s-qua-di-s-di ni-ge-sv-na gv-ni-dv i-ga-tse-li i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi na-s-gi-ya u-du-gi i-gv-sv-i.

170308.english: This is a faithful saying, and these things I will that thou affirm constantly, that they which have believed in God might be careful to maintain good works. These things are good and profitable unto men.
170308.cherokee: ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯᏯ ᎢᎦᏪᏛ; ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏧᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᎠᏆᏚᎵᎭ ᏣᏍᏓᏱᏗᏍᏗᏱ, ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏃᎯᏳᏅᎯ ᎤᎾᎦᏌᏯᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᏂᎪᎯᎸᏉ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏧᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗᏱ.  ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᎾᎵᏍᏕᎸᏙᏗ ᏴᏫ.
170308.phonetic: U-do-hi-yu-hi-ya i-ga-we-dv; a-le na-s-gi hi-a tsu-da-le-nv-dv a-qua-du-li-ha tsa-s-da-yi-di-s-di-yi, na-s-gi na U-ne-la-nv-hi u-no-hi-yu-nv-hi u-na-ga-sa-ya-s-do-di-yi ni-go-hi-lv-quo o-s-dv tsu-ni-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di-yi.  Hi-a na-s-gi o-si-yu a-le gv-wa-na-li-s-de-lv-do-di yv-wi.

170309.english: But avoid foolish questions, and genealogies, and contentions, and strivings about the law; for they are unprofitable and vain.
170309.cherokee: ᎤᎵᏍᎦᏂᏍᏛᏍᎩᏂ ᎧᏃᎮᎸᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᏓᏁᏟᏴᏒᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᏘᏲᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏗᏒᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ ᏕᎭᏓᏅᏒᎮᏍᏗ, ᎬᏩᎾᏓᏍᏕᎸᏗᏰᏃ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏎᏉᏉ ᎾᏍᎩ.
170309.phonetic: U-li-s-ga-ni-s-dv-s-gi-ni ka-no-he-lv-di ge-sv a-le u-na-da-ne-tli-yv-sv-sv-i, a-le di-ti-yo-s-di ge-sv-i, a-le a-di-sv-s-di ge-sv di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di u-gv-wa-li de-ha-da-nv-sv-he-s-di, gv-wa-na-da-s-de-lv-di-ye-no ni-ge-sv-na a-le a-se-quo-quo na-s-gi.

170310.english: A man that is an heretick after the first and second admonition reject;
170310.cherokee: ᎩᎶ ᏗᎦᎴᏅᏗᏍᎩ ᏌᏉ ᎠᎴ ᏔᎵ ᎢᏯᎨᏯᏔᏅᎯ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎡᏥᏲᎢᏎᎸᎭ;
170310.phonetic: Gi-lo di-ga-le-nv-di-s-gi sa-quo a-le ta-li i-ya-ge-ya-ta-nv-hi ge-se-s-di e-tsi-yo-i-se-lv-ha;

170311.english: Knowing that he that is such is subverted, and sinneth, being condemned of himself.
170311.cherokee: ᎢᏥᎦᏔᎮᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᏱᏄᏍᏗ ᎩᎶ ᎤᎪᎸᏛ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏍᎦᏅᏨᎢ, ᎤᏩᏒᏉ ᏓᏓᏚᎪᏓᏁᎲᎢ.
170311.phonetic: I-tsi-ga-ta-he-s-di na-s-gi na yi-nu-s-di gi-lo u-go-lv-dv ge-sv-i, a-le u-s-ga-nv-tsv-i, u-wa-sv-quo da-da-du-go-da-ne-hv-i.

170312.english: When I shall send Artemas unto thee, or Tychicus, be diligent to come unto me to Nicopolis: for I have determined there to winter.
170312.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏃ ᎠᏘᎹ ᏥᏅᏒᎭ ᏫᏣᎷᏤᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᎩᎦ, ᎭᏟᏂᎬᏁᎸᎭ ᎾᎪᏆᎵ ᏍᎩᎷᏤᏗᏱ; ᎾᎿᎭᏰᏃ ᏓᏇᎪᏔᏅ ᎠᏆᎪᎳᏗᏴᏗᏱ.
170312.phonetic: I-yu-no A-ti-ma tsi-nv-sv-ha wi-tsa-lu-tse-di-yi, a-le Di-gi-ga, ha-tli-ni-gv-ne-lv-ha Na-go-qua-li s-gi-lu-tse-di-yi; na-hna-ye-no da-que-go-ta-nv a-qua-go-la-di-yv-di-yi.

170313.english: Bring Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their journey diligently, that nothing be wanting unto them.
170313.cherokee: ᏥᎾ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᏗᎪᏏᏏᏍᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏉᎳ ᎭᏟᏂᎬᏁᎸᎭ ᎩᏍᏕᎸᏗᏱ ᎠᎾᎢᏒᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏄᏂᏂᎬᏎᎲᎾ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ.
170313.phonetic: Tsi-na di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di di-go-si-si-s-gi a-le A-quo-la ha-tli-ni-gv-ne-lv-ha Gi-s-de-lv-di-yi a-na-i-sv-i, na-s-gi go-hu-s-di nu-ni-ni-gv-se-hv-na i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi.

170314.english: And let ours also learn to maintain good works for necessary uses, that they be not unfruitful.
170314.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏗᎦᏤᎵᎦ ᏩᎾᏕᎶᏆ ᏂᎪᎯᎸ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏧᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗᏱ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏚᎸᏗ ᏂᎦᎵᏍᏗᏍᎬ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎬᏔᏂᏓᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏂᏁᏉᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᎾᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ.
170314.phonetic: A-le na-s-quo di-ga-tse-li-ga wa-na-de-lo-qua ni-go-hi-lv o-s-dv tsu-ni-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di-yi na-s-gi u-du-lv-di ni-ga-li-s-di-s-gv go-hu-s-di gv-ta-ni-da-s-di, na-s-gi a-ni-ne-quo-s-gi i-yu-na-li-s-do-di-yi.

170315.english: All that are with me salute thee. Greet them that love us in the faith. Grace be with you all. Amen.
170315.cherokee: ᏂᎦᏛ ᎠᏂ ᏃᎦᏛᏅᎢ ᏫᎨᏣᏲᎵᎦ.  ᏕᎯᏲᎵᎸᎭ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎨᎩᎨᏳᎯ ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬᎢ.  ᎬᏩᎦᏘᏯ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏤᎳᏗᏙᎮᏍᏗ ᏂᏥᎥᎢ.  ᎡᎺᏅ.
170315.phonetic: Ni-ga-dv a-ni no-ga-dv-nv-i wi-ge-tsa-yo-li-ga.  De-hi-yo-li-lv-ha na-s-gi na ge-gi-ge-yu-hi go-hi-yu-di ge-sv nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv-i.  Gv-wa-ga-ti-ya u-da-do-li-s-di ge-sv i-tse-la-di-do-he-s-di ni-tsi-v-i.  E-me-nv.
